title: calophanes radicans
intro: Calophanes radicans, also known as wild geranium, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly due to its high concentration of flavonoids and tannins, which possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Calophanes radicans is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antispasmodic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use, as well as dried for use in herbal remedies, such as salves and poultices.
